values.title: values
values.description: Repeated field of dynamically typed values.

google.protobuf.ListValue.title: ListValue
google.protobuf.ListValue.description:
`ListValue` is a wrapper around a repeated field of values.

 The JSON representation for `ListValue` is JSON array.

google.protobuf.NullValue.title: NullValue
google.protobuf.NullValue.description:
`NullValue` is a singleton enumeration to represent the null value for the
 `Value` type union.

 The JSON representation for `NullValue` is JSON `null`.

google.protobuf.Struct.description:
`Struct` represents a structured data value, consisting of fields
 which map to dynamically typed values. In some languages, `Struct`
 might be supported by a native representation. For example, in
 scripting languages like JS a struct is represented as an
 object. The details of that representation are described together
 with the proto support for the language.

 The JSON representation for `Struct` is JSON object.

google.protobuf.Value.description:
`Value` represents a dynamically typed value which can be either
 null, a number, a string, a boolean, a recursive struct value, or a
 list of values. A producer of value is expected to set one of these
 variants. Absence of any variant indicates an error.

 The JSON representation for `Value` is JSON value.

created.title: created
created.description: the timestamp of the first event applied to the object.

changed.title: changed
changed.description: the timestamp of the last event applied to the object.

owner.title: owner
owner.description: the parent object representing the returned objects context.

details.title: details
details.description: Details provide some base information (such as the last change date) of the schema.

revision.title: revision
revision.description: Revision is a read only version of the schema, each update of the `schema`-field increases the revision.

id.title: id
id.description: Defines the ID of the user schema to filter for.

method.title: method
method.description: Defines which text comparison method used for the id filter.

type.title: type
type.description: Type is a human readable word describing the schema.

possibleAuthenticators.title: possible_authenticators
possibleAuthenticators.description:
Defines the possible types of authenticators.

 Removal of an authenticator does not remove the authenticator on a user.

schema.title: schema
schema.description: JSON schema representation defining the user.

andFilter.title: and_filter
andFilter.description:
Limit the result to match all sub queries ('AND').
 Note that if you specify multiple queries, they will be implicitly used as andQueries.
 Use the andFilter in combination with orFilter and notFilter.

idFilter.title: id_filter
idFilter.description: Limit the result to a specific schema ID.

notFilter.title: not_filter
notFilter.description: Exclude / Negate the result of the sub filter ('NOT').

orFilter.title: or_filter
orFilter.description: Union the results of each sub filter ('OR').

stateFilter.title: state_filter
stateFilter.description: Limit the result to a specific state of the schema.

typeFilter.title: type_filter
typeFilter.description: Limit the result to a specific schema type.

state.title: state
state.description: Defines the state to filter for.

type.title: type
type.description: Defines which type to filter for.

method.title: method
method.description: Defines which text comparison method used for the type filter.

type.title: type
type.description: Type is a human readable word describing the schema.

possibleAuthenticators.title: possible_authenticators
possibleAuthenticators.description: Defines the possible types of authenticators.

schema.title: schema
schema.description: JSON schema representation defining the user.
